content.text: Have you ever wondered why the B2B
marketing playbooks from 2023 are
completely failing right now? Well, we
just tested something that got us higher
quality leads with half the work, and
the companies still playing the old game
are getting crushed. So, now look, I
know what you're thinking. It's another
AI will change everything video. I'm
going to show you the five new rules of
B2B marketing in 2026. What's actually
working, what died, and why most B2B
marketers are about to get left behind.
Rule that died. Number one, volume over
quality. The old game was to blast
hundreds of thousands of cold emails and
get maybe 100 replies or so. That worked
in 2020, but it's broken in 2026.
Everyone's inbox is flooded and open
ranks are going down. They're continuing
to tank right now. So, what we tested
instead was that we're still sending the
hundreds of thousands of emails, but
we're very targeted towards our total
addressable market. So, for us, that's
B2B SAS. And so, in that world, you
don't have a lot of people that we're
looking to target. But the other key
thing here is you can't just send
volume. The quality piece that we're
doing is one, we have a really good
offer. The really good offer is we send
an Amazon gift card. So $105 for Amazon
gift card. The reason it's $105 is
because the extra $5 gets their
attention. It's not just $100. That's a
right a nice round number. It's $105.
Okay. So it's like, oh wow, you're
incentivizing me to get on this call. I
would like to have $100 extra for the
time I'm already putting into my day
job. And but the other piece too, so we
have a product called ClickFlow, which
is our answer engine optimization, our
search engine optimization product. will
say, "Not only are we going to give you
this $15 Amazon gift card, if it makes
sense, we will give you 10,000 words for
free as well." How does that sound?
That's a no-brainer offer. You want to
make them feel so stupid for not taking
the offer. And that's the hallmark of a
good offer. Roll that died. Number two,
logging into a SAS solution and doing
everything on your own. Now, in 2026,
everyone wants a push a button and leads
appear type of thing. A one-click
marketing solution. And a lot of
marketing solutions currently are just
simply reporting tools. You have to log
in and track your ranking. You have to
look at how you're stacking up to your
competitors. It's not exactly the best.
I want something that tells me how I'm
doing right now. Comes up with some
recommendations and just does it for me.
That's going to make you a lot more
money automatically because you're going
to log into Cursor and it's going to
say, "Hey, we got all these insights for
you. We're recommending these changes.
Okay, you select the ones that you like.
Do you want us to just do it for you?"
Boom. When you say yes, you just verify
that what the work looks like is good.
That makes work a lot easier. Rule that
died. Number three, traditional SEO as
the main channel. So, website traffic is
continuing to decline. We're talking 20,
30, 40, 50% dips. When you look at AI
overviews, Google's AI overviews,
they're eating up 30 to 40% of the
clicks. And you look at traffic on our
website at least, most websites right
now, it's a open jaw traffic pattern,
meaning that the impressions are going
up, but the clicks are going down. Don't
get me wrong, Google still matters.
Google, in fact, they're still one of
the most profitable companies in the
world. $116 billion in profit. I think
the most profitable company. But you got
to look at this as website traffic that
you have coming in is a cigar butt
channel, meaning that that traffic is
going to continue to decline over time.
Google is still going to have more
queries. People are searching for more
things. And especially when you're using
AI, the queries are a lot longer. People
are searching for a lot more things. And
so usage is is going to go up, but just
expect that your website traffic is
going to continue to decline. Meaning
that traditional SEO as we know it is
slowly dying. I hate to say it, but me
coming in with the SEO background is
slowly dying. What you got to look at
instead is SEO as search everywhere
optimization. And meaning that you look
at some of the traffic being diverted
over into Chat GBT, Perplexity, Claude,
Gemini. Sure, those LLMs are available.
But when I say search everywhere
optimization, that means YouTube, second
largest search engine in the world. That
means if if you're optimizing for
shoppers, Amazon, maybe you're doing
Pinterest as well, maybe you're doing
app store optimization. There's a lot of
different options now. And attention is
so desperate. When I first started my
career in SEO, we're talking 15 plus
years ago or so. SEO was very much the
only game in town. Instagram wasn't even
really taking off yet. YouTube was was
still kind of very nent. The game has
changed quite a bit and you've got to
adapt and you can't just bet on Google
SEO because if you just do that, you're
going to be screwed. So internally for
the single grain team now for our
internal marketing we're this is why I'm
recording this video right now. This is
going to go to YouTube. We're going to
try to figure out how to chop it up for
short form clips as well. This pillar
topic over here can now move over to
LinkedIn. It can move over to X as well.
We're trying to play the everywhere game
because organic reach is no longer just
constraint to one channel. And back in
the day when you look at marketing they
like to used to say that marketing was
the rule of seven because there's so
many different channels now. Marketing
is very much the rule of 21. There's
actually numbers to back this up now.
Even the rule of 28 in some cases,
right? Because when you pull out your
phone, as an example, oh, you might be
looking at X and then you're you're
scrolling Tik Tok and you're scrolling
Instagram. So, you have a lot of
different options. Now, when we talk to
clients, the ones who are trying the
everywhere game and they're listening to
this content, they're the ones that are
actually thriving. The ones that just
want to do SEO, they just want to do
traditional SEO with link building,
keyword research, you know, blog
content. Not saying that stuff doesn't
work, but you have to enhance and expand
your strategy. New rule number one is
adoption speed over strategy. So, the
Fiverr CEO quote said this, "If you're
not adopting AI, you are now a burden on
society." And my belief is, look, that's
harsh, but it's true. There are
companies, like my friend's company,
they're hiring a bunch, actually, a
bunch of 17 to 19 year olds who actually
will volunteer to sleep in the office.
These are kids that have grown up with
AI, and they don't know a world where AI
hasn't existed. And they're constantly
asking, "How can I do more? We should do
things this way. This way of doing
things is stupid. They don't understand
how we have done things over the last, I
don't know, 20, 30 years or so." So, the
level of motivation is skyhigh. Plus,
the AI fluency is sky-high as well. And
so if you can, maybe 20% of the hires
that you're going for, you're betting on
potential. You're betting on these kids
that are super AI fluent and have a very
high motor to get things done. So if
your team isn't adopting AI, they don't
belong in this new world, at least at
your company, if you're trying to move
quickly and you're trying to do big
things. New rule number two, human plus
AI operator model. You're not
necessarily looking to just replace
humans. You're looking to become an AI
operator managing agents. And that's
what you're trying to teach your team to
do. You want to replace those humans
with humans that are AI operators. And
that sounds crude, but the way I see it
is it's akin to not knowing how to use
power tools. If you're building a house,
it's akin to not using a computer today.
Like, why would you not use a computer?
You use a computer right now cuz you can
do your work a lot faster. You use the
internet cuz you can do your work a lot
faster. And so, not using AI means
you're handicapping yourself. The world
that we're moving into right now is is a
world in which one person can become a
100x employee. So, that one person can
build one agentic workflow and then
stack a bunch more. And so, here's an
example. At my company, it's very
important for us to see what is the
number one thing that someone got done
yesterday. What's the number one thing
that they're working on today? That's
actually very important because the
number one thing when you're hiring
someone is you want to make sure that
they're working on the right thing,
they're prioritizing on the right thing
because you have a vision. You have a
mission for the company. And so, we
built an agentic workflow that will look
at what people are entering in for what
they're working on. And then we have a
coach that will coach them on, hey, is
this the highest leverage thing that you
can work on? Or maybe you should be
working on this instead, maybe you
should be thinking about this instead.
And that worked really well for us when
we first launched it. The key thing is
when you launch these things, you want
to make sure that you're continuing to
iterate on these. One person now can
become a 100x employee. And when we take
that into a B2B marketing context,
there's one example of a agentic
workflow that we built. When we have
leads come in to our website, we use
Perplexity to enhance those leads. What
is it that they're looking for? What
does that person do? What is this
person's tenure? What is this person
interested in? So, look at their ex. So,
look at their LinkedIn as well. And that
data, that enrichment will then be
serviced over to our sales rep that will
then get on the call with them. And then
they'll realize, hey, what is this
person's title? How is this person
trended over time? What are they
interested in? So that sales rep can
build more of a relationship with that
person. And that increases our
conversion rates because it shows that
we've actually done our research. That's
a very simple example of a sales
enrichment agentic workflow that you can
build. And you can use different
products out there to do this for you
such as Lindy or Zapier. So new rule
number three. I kind of talked about
this earlier, but cross-platform
authority. So not a single channel move
because back in the day, you could have
just done SEO, but you got to be
everywhere now. Your authority might be
your blog, your YouTube, your Reddit,
your LinkedIn. And all these AI
platforms will look at your entire
digital presence, not just one website
in isolation, which can be a good or bad
thing. And so ultimately, what you want
to think about is ecosystem, not single
pages. New rule number four, B2B CTV. So
we're talking about connected TV ads.
This is mispriced right now. So we've
been running B2B CTV ads. So they're not
very competitive right now. Inventory is
great. It's very affordable and the ROI
is way higher than B TOC. Most B2B
companies stick to tried and true.
They've never really tried the original
new stuff. And if you're willing to take
a shot, carve out a 10 to 15% fund
budget. There's a multi-trillion dollar
company that we're talking to right now
that thinks exactly like this. We just
spoke with them last week and they said,
you know, 10% is our fun budget. We want
to try new things. And I talked to
another company. This is a multi-billion
dollar company. And they're like, "Yeah,
we're trying new things all the time. We
want to try influencer. We want to try
channels like appleven. We want to try
new things." Right? So just keep in mind
that uh we're talking about CTV but
ultimately what we're talking about is
trying new things and experimenting
because most of your experiments 90% are
not going to work but 10% will and that
10% is going to drive a lot more growth
than your triedand-true channels in the
future. New rule number five creatorled
B2B LinkedIn plus content. So yesterday
I just did a webinar with uh Adam
Robinson. So Adam Robinson is one of my
friends. He has a company called RB2B.
Uh he drives all of his pipeline for his
7 million ARR company called RB2B. He
just has a lot of LinkedIn posts that he
does. Being able to drive all of your
pipeline from LinkedIn is very powerful.
So when you're creatorled B2B like Adam
Robinson or he can even hit other
YouTube channels as well. There's a lot
of great examples around this. Like I
have companies such as Meta, Snap,
Adobe, hit me up to do influencer deals.
So the number one benefit of being a B2B
creator is that you're able to drive
pipeline and real business for whatever
it is that you're trying to build,
whether it's a product or a service.
That's the first thing. Now the second
piece is creating content is very
expensive. And so I know for us at the
very least right now we're spending
about $22,000 a month on content which
is way too expensive right now. It is
way too much money and we're struggling
to see the ROI on it. But if you're
struggling to see the direct ROI from
all the work that you spend on content,
what I will say is that business
creators are getting lots of pitches.
I'll give you an example. There are
publicly traded companies that have paid
me anywhere from multiple five figures
to do one video. There's like two or
three that hit me up this week and
they're willing to do deals and when we
tell them immediately fee is $25,000 to
just try to push them away. oftentimes a
lot of them will say, "Hey, we can do
it." Right? I talked to someone recently
who works at a PR company. They work
with Meta to to work with influencers.
And Meta is often willing to spend $50
to $100,000 to work with influencers. So
my point of saying this is that sure
creating content as a B2B operator is
expensive. That's number one. But you
can offset those costs as you start to
grow using these influencer deals. And
but the other piece too is that you are
going to drive pipeline. And this
pipeline compounds over time, too. And
so you don't want to sleep on living
this new world of being a B2B creator
because it works really well. Now, that
being said, influencer deals can offset
the cost. But the way you're going to
make the most money when you have more
reach over time is, let's say you're
making a YouTube video, you want to have
mid rolls in there where you're
promoting your own stuff. That's where
you're going to have your best ROI. But
I think you can do both. I used to be of
the mindset that you shouldn't take
these brand deals, but now I'm like,
okay, the content costs are just so much
right now that we're getting to the
point where we're just like, hey, should

costs? But if you do these influencer
deals, you can offset them. So that's
how you can play this game. Real quick,
if you are looking for a new gig and

All right, so we talked about a lot.
Here's what you actually need to do
Monday morning. If you're a $10 million

themselves. Have one team dedicated to

you have to make sure that you hire

going to try to cut corners cuz they're
they're always going to want to throw

that means you're opening up your

if you're ironfisted about AI, this is a
really easy way to see people that who
want to learn, right? And then we got to
talk about the speed advantage because
most marketers right now, they're still

faster now. All the keywords, backlinks,

that didn't have a lot of thought. Not
saying they don't work today, but they
they require a lot more thought and the
meta, the the most optimized way of
playing has changed. So, you've got 6 to
12 months before everyone else catches
up. Same thing happened with SEO and
early days. Early movers capture 10x
value. You don't want to wait. You want

just got to move a lot faster. The way I
see it with B2B marketing now combined

before everyone else catches up. The
same thing happened early days with SEO.
The ones that moved very quickly, they
accrewed a lot of traffic, a lot of

you don't want to wait right now. When
you look at things such as chatkit

are a lot of opportunities coming with
B2B marketing and it's very exciting if
you move quickly. So those are the five
new rules of B2B marketing in 2026.
quality over volume, AI operators,
